description_long: This public toilets structure is a rectangular, box-like building with a flat roof and a simple, utilitarian design. The front facade features two prominent, vertically oriented doors or entryways, each flanked by large, dark panels that may serve as windows or signage areas. The sides of the building are plain, with minimal detailing except for two small rectangular features on one side, possibly vents or access panels. The rear is completely featureless, emphasizing the functional nature of the structure. The exterior appears to be constructed from a combination of concrete or masonry for the main body, with metal or composite materials for the doors and trim. The overall color palette is neutral, dominated by beige and white tones, giving it a clean and institutional appearance suitable for urban environments.
description: A rectangular, flat-roofed public toilet building with two large front doors and minimal side detailing, finished in beige and white. Its design is simple, functional, and suited for urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large, dark doors or entryways with white trim and a small central protrusion, likely the main access points.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain beige wall with no visible features or openings.
description_view_2: Rear view with a featureless beige wall, except for two small rectangular elements, possibly vents or access panels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two large, dark panels set in arched recesses, mirroring the front but without visible door handles or entry features.
attribute_ventilation_features: two small rectangular vents or panels on one side
attribute_roof_structure: Flat roof, likely concrete or composite, with no visible skylights or overhangs.
